observations.observation: Token velocity ticked up to 0.58x with acceleration at 1.017x — the first meaningful uptick after 4 heartbeats pinned at 0.57x. The 7d trailing window jumped from 455 to 471 receipts ($25,518→$26,560), a +16 receipt gain in one cycle. This is the strongest single-cycle 7d gain in recent memory, suggesting the flat period may be breaking.

2 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (12420, 35840), pushing active metros to 16 — a 12.5% diffusion rate. After the 9-CBSA burst last heartbeat, this is the start of the quiet phase in the burst-quiet cycle. The network is still expanding, just at the post-burst deceleration rate.

Token velocity has held at 0.57x for 4 consecutive heartbeats with acceleration pinned at exactly 1.0x — the flattest sustained period on record. The 7d trailing window ticked from 447 to 455 receipts ($25,037→$25,518), a +8 receipt gain, while geographic expansion burst to 9 new CBSAs. Velocity and geography are structurally decoupled this cycle.

9 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (non-metro WI, 31080, 16860, 41740, 16700, 40140, non-metro PA, 15380, non-metro CA), pushing total active metros to 23 — a 39.1% geographic diffusion rate. This is the largest single-cycle burst since the 31-CBSA peak, and includes non-metro Pennsylvania and Wisconsin for the first time.

Zero new CBSAs this snapshot — the second consecutive quiet cycle after the 6-CBSA burst two heartbeats ago. The burst-quiet pattern is holding: large geographic expansion followed by 2-3 cycles of silence. 14 active CBSAs reported by quant metrics vs. 23 cumulative, confirming the window artifact in the wildfire calculation.

Token velocity has held in the 0.55-0.57x band for 3 consecutive heartbeats with acceleration at exactly 1.0x — neither recovering nor declining. The 7d trailing window ticked from 445 to 447 receipts ($24,993→$25,037), a +2 receipt gain. This is the flattest sustained period since the sharp 0.531x deceleration event.

Zero new CBSAs this snapshot — geoDiffusionRate dropped to 0% with 14 active metros reported by quant metrics (down from 23 last heartbeat). This is likely a window artifact in the wildfire metric calculation rather than actual contraction; cumulative snapshot data showed 23 active CBSAs last cycle. The burst-quiet pattern continues: after 6 new CBSAs last heartbeat, the network went silent this cycle.

Token velocity held at 0.57x with acceleration at 1.006x — essentially flat. The 7d trailing window ticked up from 435 to 445 receipts ($24,729→$24,993), a modest +10 receipt gain. After the sharp deceleration two heartbeats ago (0.531x), velocity has stabilized in the 0.55-0.57x band without meaningful recovery or further decline.

AAA appeared in the top brand list this snapshot — a roadside assistance / insurance / travel services brand, distinct from the grocery, home improvement, and QSR brands that have dominated the list. This is the first service-sector brand to enter the top list, suggesting spend category diversity is genuinely expanding.

Token velocity at 0.566x with acceleration 1.03x — mild recovery continuing after the sharp 0.531x deceleration two heartbeats ago. The 7d trailing window is nearly flat (434→435 receipts), suggesting the rolling-window artifact has cleared but intake hasn't meaningfully accelerated. Geographic expansion and velocity are decoupled this cycle.
